legal_basis: Taxation Administration Act 1953 (TAA) Schedule 1, Div 396; Income Tax Assessment Act 1997; Common Reporting Standard (CRS) Regulations 2017
reporting_deadline: 31 July following the 31 December reporting year-end
submission_method: ATO Online Services; also ATO Small Reporter Tool for FIs with few reportable accounts
nil_report_required: Yes - CRS703 nil report required if Reporting FI has zero reportable accounts
data_retention_years: 12 months post-submission (ATO requirement); underlying records 5 years minimum
doc_ref_id_format: AU[ReportingYear]AU[ABN_or_GIIN_or_TFN][UniqueRef] - all uppercase, no spaces or special characters
local_fi_scope: Authorised Deposit-taking Institutions (ADIs): banks, building societies, credit unions
Life insurance companies offering investment-linked products
Superannuation funds (most are Non-Reporting; check ATO list)
Trustee-documented trusts where trustee is Reporting FI
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
Superannuation accounts of registered superannuation entities (specifically exempt)
local_specific_rules: TRUST REPORTING: A trust that is an Investment Entity must be reported. The Trustee is the Reporting FI. Trustee must obtain self-cert from trust beneficial owners.
NIL REPORTS: Mandatory using CRS703 MessageTypeIndic. Failure to lodge nil report is a compliance failure.
ABN FORMAT IN DocRefId: Use the 11-digit ABN (no spaces) as the FI identifier in DocRefId where no GIIN exists.
SMALL REPORTER TOOL: ATO provides an Excel-based tool for FIs with fewer reportable accounts who cannot generate XML. Converts to XML for submission.
AGGREGATION RULE: All accounts held by same account holder at same FI must be aggregated for threshold testing, including accounts held by related entities if FI systems can identify them.
CHANGE OF CIRCUMSTANCES: FI must update account classification within 90 days of becoming aware of a change; may not close as non-reportable until verified.
DATA RETENTION: ATO requires records to be kept for at least 12 months after the CRS report is submitted, in addition to general 5-year business record requirement.

rejection_error_codes.resolution: Check if previously submitted; use OECD2 to correct

rejection_error_codes.desc: CorrDocRefId not found - original DocRefId does not exist
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Verify original DocRefId was successfully accepted
Validate TIN format per OECD TIN guidance; use unknown if not held
Ensure FI ABN or GIIN is correctly formatted in ReportingFI element
Provide account number; use IBAN or internal reference if no formal account number
Use ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format; use YYYY-01-01 if only year known
Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code; verify reportable jurisdiction list

rejection_error_codes.desc: Schema validation failure - missing mandatory element
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Run XML against OECD CRS XSD before submission

penalties.failure_to_report: Penalty under TAA s.288-25: up to AUD 55,000 per failure for entities
penalties.false_or_misleading_statement: TAA s.284-75: up to 75% of tax shortfall
penalties.late_lodgment: ATO may issue penalty notice; first offence usually remitted on request

fatca.portal: ATO Online Services (same portal as CRS)
key_rules: Australia is Model 1A: Australian FIs report FATCA data to ATO, which passes to IRS
US persons identified by 7 indicia (US citizenship/residence, US birthplace, US address, US phone, US standing instruction, US power of attorney, US in-care-of address)
Recalcitrant account: if no self-cert from US person, report as recalcitrant with balance only
Aggregation same as CRS for threshold testing

gaps: Exact rejection error code numbers should be verified against current ATO Online Services documentation
Small Reporter Tool version/availability should be verified at time of reporting
